violations.text: Clear Chowder, which was prepared previous day, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours (Observed at 46-48 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler). Based on temperature observed, chowder was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling time and temperature, and strategies were discussed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of the person in charge, Clear Chowder was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Cooling strategies were discussed at the time of inspection... (1) Placing the FOOD in shallow pans (2) Separating the FOOD into smaller or thinner portions (3) Using rapid cooling EQUIPMENT (Freezer, Ice Wands) (4) Stirring the food in a container placed in an ice water bath (5) Using containers that facilitate heat transfer (6) Adding ice as an ingredient New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

At the time of inspection, Pico de Gallo and Mango Salsa were observed cold held in an ice bath at 45 and 48 degrees Fahrenheit respectively. Per the PIC, the Pico de Gallo and the Mango Salsa were recently placed into the ice bath from cold refrigeration. At the time of inspection, the ice bath was observed to be insufficient to maintain proper cold holding temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. PIC was instructed to use a larger pan with extra water and ice for a proper "Ice Slurry". Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

At the time of inspection, hanging fly tape was observed being used as a pest control device in the food prep/storage area. Insect control devices shall be installed so that the devices are not located over a FOOD preparation area, and Dead insects and insect fragments are prevented from being impelled onto or falling on exposed FOOD; clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, and LINENS; and unwrapped SINGLESERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

The following equipment were observed to be in poor repair... - Gaskets to the Cold Drawer unit - Ice Machine (Currently not in use. Per the PIC, Work Order currently in place for the Ice Machine. PIC purchasing bagged ice from approved source.) New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris at the time of inspection... - Refrigeration Unit Gaskets - Cold Drawer slides - Slushy Mixer Unit - Utensil/Equipment shelving in the warewashing area - New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2023

The door to the toilet room located in the kitchen/storage area is not self-closing. Except where a toilet room is located outside a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT and does not open directly into the FOOD ESTABLISHMENT, a toilet room located on the PREMISES shall be completely enclosed and provided with a tight-fitting and self-closing door. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2023
